sanskrit_devanagari: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
sanskrit_iast: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi ||
english_meaning: You have the right to perform your duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to inaction.
themes: doing what is right regardless of results

sanskrit_devanagari: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते। क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप॥
sanskrit_iast: klaibyaṃ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate | kṣudraṃ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṃ tyaktvottiṣṭha parantapa ||
english_meaning: Do not yield to this weakness of heart, O Partha. It does not become you. Cast off this petty faint-heartedness and arise, O scorcher of foes.
themes: the voice that does not let you give up

sanskrit_devanagari: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्। मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्॥
sanskrit_iast: tasmāt tvam uttiṣṭha yaśo labhasva jitvā śatrūn bhuṅkṣva rājyaṃ samṛddham | mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva nimitta-mātraṃ bhava savyasācin ||
english_meaning: Therefore arise and win glory. Conquer your enemies and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. These warriors are already slain by Me. You are merely an instrument, O Savyasachin.
themes: accepting your role in something larger
being an instrument not the author

sanskrit_devanagari: श्रीगुरु चरन सरोज रज निज मनु मुकुरु सुधारि। बरनउँ रघुबर बिमल जसु जो दायकु फल चारि॥
sanskrit_iast: śrī-guru carana saroja raja nija manu mukuru sudhāri | baranauṃ raghubara bimala jasu jo dāyaku phala cāri ||
english_meaning: Having cleansed the mirror of my mind with the dust of my Guru's lotus feet, I sing the pure glory of Raghuvara, the bestower of the four fruits of life.

sanskrit_devanagari: राम दुआरे तुम रखवारे। होत न आज्ञा बिनु पैसारे॥
sanskrit_iast: rāma duāre tuma rakhavāre | hota na ājñā binu paisāre ||
english_meaning: You are the guardian at Rama's door. No one may enter without your permission.
themes: the gatekeeper who serves out of love
